claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under the age of 16, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex, who is 15 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have Alex, who is 15 years old.

**My next question is: Do you currently live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. That's helpful information.

**Next question: Are you a British citizen, or do you have any specific immigration status I should be aware of (such as settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or have you recently moved to the UK)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for confirming that.

**One more question to complete my assessment: Is Alex currently in paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or receiving any benefits such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work and isn't receiving any benefits.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old
- You live with Alex in the UK (in Manchester)
- You are a British citizen
- Alex is not in paid work (24+ hours per week) and is not receiving benefits

You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. You are responsible for Alex (as you live with them), Alex is under 16, you live in the UK, and there are no disqualifying factors preventing you from claiming.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.** 

You can now go ahead and make a claim if you haven't already done so. If you have any further questions about Child Benefit, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex. The claimant is a British citizen and there are no disqualifying factors - Alex is not in paid work for 24+ hours per week and is not receiving benefits that would stop Child Benefit eligibility. Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester with their 15-year-old child Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: the claimant is responsible for the child (living with them), the child is under 16, they reside in the UK, and there are no disqualifying circumstances. The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex."]

Therefore, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
